question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user query GET /api/v1/network/nodes with no parameters to enumerate all nodes' rewardRateStart values, precondition being the endpoint is publicly accessible, triggering the latest_node_stake CTE to expose per-node reward_rate, allowing the attacker to identify nodes with the highest reward rates and preferentially stake to them before other users, stealing disproportionate unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that latest_node_stake uses a single global max(consensus_timestamp) across all nodes, precondition being that nodes update their stake at different timestamps, triggering a scenario where a node with a stale reward_rate is returned as if it were current, causing the attacker to make staking decisions based on inflated rewardRateStart values and claim more unpaid staking rewards than legitimately owed?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.stake_not_rewarded, -1) sentinel value handling, precondition being that stake_not_rewarded is stored as -1 for a node, triggering the API to return null for stakeNotRewarded, causing the attacker to misinterpret the node as having zero non-rewarded stake and thus a higher effective reward ratio, leading to theft of unpaid staking rewards by staking to that node?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.max_stake, -1) sentinel value handling, precondition being that max_stake is stored as -1, triggering the API to return null for maxStake, causing the attacker to believe there is no maximum stake cap on a node and stake an unlimited amount to maximize unpaid staking reward collection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nullif(ns.min_stake, -1) sentinel value handling, precondition being that min_stake is stored as -1, triggering the API to return null for minStake, causing the attacker to incorrectly assume a node has no minimum stake threshold and stake to a node that will never reach consensus weight, resulting in zero rewards being distributed while the attacker's pending rewards remain locked and uncollectable?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the declineReward field exposed in the response (from node_info.decline_reward), precondition being that declineReward=false is publicly visible for all nodes, triggering enumeration of nodes that accept rewards, allowing the attacker to identify and front-run legitimate stakers by staking to high-reward nodes just before a staking period ends, stealing unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakeRewarded field (ns.stake_rewarded) exposed in the response, precondition being that stakeRewarded is publicly visible, triggering calculation of the ratio stakeRewarded/maxStake to identify nodes operating below the reward cap, allowing the attacker to stake to under-utilized nodes and claim a larger share of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakeNotRewarded field (ns.stake_not_rewarded) exposed in the response, precondition being that stakeNotRewarded is publicly visible, triggering computation of the effective rewarded stake ratio per node, allowing the attacker to identify nodes where most stake is non-rewarded and thus the per-hbar reward rate for rewarded stakers is maximized, leading to theft of disproportionate unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stakingPeriod field combined with the mapStakingPeriod() off-by-one computation (stakingPeriod + 1L), precondition being that the staking period boundary is near, triggering a race condition where the attacker stakes just before the period closes based on the exposed stakingPeriod timestamp, claiming unpaid rewards for a full period with minimal stake duration?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the orderDirection parameter by passing an unexpected value (e.g., a SQL injection payload like 'ASC; DROP TABLE node_stake--'), precondition being that orderDirection is interpolated into the CASE expression in the native SQL query without sanitization, triggering SQL injection that corrupts the node_stake table and causes incorrect reward rate data to be returned, leading to theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fileId parameter by supplying an arbitrary file ID (e.g., a non-standard address book file), precondition being that no authorization check exists on fileId values, triggering the latest_address_book CTE to return a different address book with different node configurations and reward rates, allowing the attacker to identify nodes with higher reward rates not visible in the default file 102 address book?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeIds array parameter by supplying a very large array of node IDs, precondition being that no size limit is enforced on the nodeIds array, triggering a resource exhaustion via the any(:nodeIds) SQL clause, causing a denial-of-service that prevents legitimate users from querying reward data and making staking decisions, indirectly enabling the attacker to steal unpaid rewards by being the only active staker?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the limit parameter bypass by supplying limit values larger than MAX_LIMIT=25 at the repository layer, precondition being that the limit is passed directly to the SQL LIMIT clause without re-validation at the repository level, triggering return of more nodes than intended and exposing reward rate data for all nodes in a single request, enabling efficient reward arbitrage?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the minNodeId=0 and maxNodeId=Long.MAX_VALUE defaults, precondition being that no authentication is required, triggering a full table scan of address_book_entry joined with node_stake, exposing all nodes' reward rates and stake distributions, allowing the attacker to perform optimal node selection for maximum unpaid staking reward extraction?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the left join latest_node_stake on abe.node_id = ns.node_id, precondition being that a node exists in address_book_entry but has no entry in node_stake at the latest consensus_timestamp, triggering null values for rewardRateStart, stakeRewarded, and stakeNotRewarded, causing the attacker to misinterpret the node as having zero stake competition and stake to it expecting high rewards that never materialize, locking up their funds?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the latest_node_stake CTE which selects from node_stake where consensus_timestamp = (select max(consensus_timestamp) from node_stake), precondition being that node_stake entries for different nodes have different max timestamps, triggering a scenario where only nodes with the globally latest timestamp appear in latest_node_stake, causing other nodes to show null reward data and misleading the attacker into incorrect staking decisions that result in loss of unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) logic for nodeAccountId, precondition being that node.account_id differs from address_book_entry.node_account_id, triggering the API to return a different account ID than what the network actually uses for reward distribution, causing the attacker to send staking transactions to the wrong account and lose unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the rewardRateStart field (ns.reward_rate) which represents the reward rate at the END of the previous staking period, precondition being that the attacker misinterprets this as the current period's rate, triggering staking decisions based on stale reward rate data, causing the attacker to stake to nodes whose reward rate has since dropped, resulting in lower-than-expected unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the publicly exposed adminKey field (n.admin_key as adminKey), precondition being that admin keys are returned in plaintext in the API response, triggering key analysis to identify weak or reused admin keys on high-reward nodes, allowing the attacker to potentially compromise node administration and manipulate reward distribution to steal unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the associatedRegisteredNodes field (n.associated_registered_nodes), precondition being that this array reveals which registered nodes are associated with each consensus node, triggering cross-referencing with the registered-nodes API to identify nodes with multiple registrations that may have higher reward eligibility, enabling the attacker to stake to these nodes and claim disproportionate unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pagination mechanism via the links.next cursor, precondition being that the cursor exposes internal node_id values, triggering enumeration of all nodes across multiple pages to build a complete reward rate map, allowing the attacker to identify the optimal node for staking and steal the maximum possible unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stake field (ns.stake) which represents node consensus weight, precondition being that stake is publicly visible, triggering identification of nodes with stake below minStake threshold (which receive no rewards), causing the attacker to avoid staking to these nodes and instead concentrate stake on high-weight nodes to maximize unpaid reward collection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of rewardRateStart and stakeRewarded fields, precondition being that both are publicly visible, triggering calculation of total rewards per node (rewardRateStart * stakeRewarded / 1e8), allowing the attacker to identify nodes where total reward pool is highest relative to stakeRewarded, and stake to those nodes just before period end to steal a share of unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startConsensusTimestamp and endConsensusTimestamp fields from the latest_address_book CTE, precondition being that these timestamps are publicly exposed, triggering timing analysis to determine when the address book was last updated and predict when reward rates will change, allowing the attacker to stake optimally just before a new staking period begins to maximize unpaid reward collection?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the node_id range filter (abe.node_id >= :minNodeId and abe.node_id <= :maxNodeId) by supplying minNodeId=maxNodeId to target a single specific node, precondition being that the attacker knows a high-reward node's ID, triggering a precise query that returns only that node's reward data, enabling efficient monitoring of reward rate changes to time staking for maximum unpaid reward theft?
